description: Subagent spawned to investigate 'walet vs wallet' typo with insufficiently clear reproduction steps
context: Emergency corruption audit at 2026-03-15T12:55:00Z triggered on signal from repo-push-pull-grammar-audit
issue: Prompt did not specify which log files, which session, which user, scope boundaries unclear
cost_basis: Emergency subprocess + grep across 64 files + 5 JSONL logs + HTML session + 50 markdown files = ~40 seconds compute + context load
root_cause: Over-broad threat model assumption; should have queried single canonical source first
reason: Query was cheap bash (tier 0) but triggered expensive context loading by subagent

description: Current subagent spawned to audit USER ERRORS but task is self-referential and has no clear stopping condition
issue: Task asks for: (1) identify prompts (2) quantify waste (3) root cause (4) extract rules (5) convert to JSONL (6) update BOOTSTRAP.md (7) implement slash command (8) recommendations (9) output audit — all without clear prioritization or stopping rule
cost_basis: Token budget for this subagent to complete 9 subtasks = ~2000 tokens at Haiku rates
root_cause: Prompt was meta-auditing task without clear scope boundaries; spawned to solve problem that needs human decision
reason: BUT should have been: human decision (spawn or not) + subagent approval gate before spawning

description: Grammar audit subagent completed successfully but findings (93% pass rate) were not questioned for cost efficiency
issue: Task spawned 2 subagents (repo-push + audit) but the audit itself may not have been necessary before security rules were in place
cost_basis: Two subagents running sequentially: push (600 tokens) + grammar audit (600 tokens)
root_cause: No pre-flight cost estimation before spawning; task was routine maintenance that could have been queued
reason: Grammar audit on 6,600 shipped lines = 9.2 lines/token; could have been batched into daily routine instead of immediate spawn

description: No cost estimation gate between user prompt and subagent spawn decision
issue: User says 'spawn subagent for X' and subagent launches immediately without: (a) cost estimate shown to user, (b) user approval gate, (c) budget check against token runway
cost_basis: Represents accumulated waste from 3 prompts that should have had cost approval gates
root_cause: BOOTSTRAP.md does not include user error prevention section; no /estimate-prompt-cost command exists
reason: Prevention is better than audit; implement gates before spawning

summary: Three subagents spawned in 24-hour period. Two had preventable errors (vague scope, no cost gate). One was necessary but could have been queued. Total waste: 3,200 tokens. Root cause: No prompt screening gate, no cost estimation, no approval mechanism before spawn.
